name: Elena Vasquez-Torres
chief_complaint: I don't really remember what happened. My coworkers say I was shaking all over and passed out at my desk. When I woke up I was confused and my tongue is bleeding.
onset_description: Witnessed generalized tonic-clonic seizure at workplace approximately 45 minutes ago. Coworker reports sudden onset of whole-body stiffening followed by rhythmic jerking lasting approximately 2 minutes. Patient was unresponsive during the event. Post-ictally confused for approximately 15 minutes, gradually becoming more oriented. No prior seizure history.

witnessed_seizure.description: Coworker witness: 'She suddenly got stiff all over, her eyes rolled back, and then she started shaking really hard. It lasted maybe two minutes. She was making a gurgling sound. After it stopped she was out of it for a while.'
witnessed_seizure.character: generalized tonic-clonic: initial tonic phase (stiffening) followed by clonic phase (rhythmic jerking)

tongue_laceration.description: My tongue hurts really bad, I think I bit it. It's bleeding.
tongue_laceration.character: lateral tongue bite, left side, actively oozing

post_ictal_confusion.description: When I came to, I didn't know where I was or what happened. Things are getting clearer now but I still feel foggy.
post_ictal_confusion.onset: immediately after seizure, lasting approximately 15 minutes, now improving

headache.description: I have a really bad headache now, it's throbbing everywhere

muscle_soreness.description: My whole body aches like I ran a marathon, especially my arms and legs and back

urinary_incontinence.description: I wet myself during it... I'm so embarrassed. That's never happened before.
urinary_incontinence.character: loss of bladder control during tonic-clonic activity

labs.CBC: WBC 11.8 (mildly elevated post-ictal stress response), Hgb 12.5, Plt 280
labs.BMP: Na 138, K 4.2, Cr 0.7, Glucose 165 (elevated - post-ictal stress response), BUN 12, Ca 9.0, Mg 1.6 (low-normal)
labs.CK: 450 U/L (elevated from muscle contractions during seizure)
labs.prolactin: 42 ng/mL (elevated - supports generalized seizure, drawn within 30 min)
labs.urinalysis: Yellow, trace blood (likely from incontinence collection), otherwise normal, beta-hCG negative

social_history.alcohol: Drinks socially, 1-2 glasses of wine on weekends. Denies binge drinking.
social_history.drugs: Denies any illicit drug use. Has never used recreational drugs.
social_history.occupation: Graphic designer at an advertising firm, works at a computer all day
social_history.living_situation: Lives with her boyfriend in an apartment
social_history.exercise: Goes to spin class twice a week, hikes on weekends occasionally
social_history.diet: Generally balanced, vegetarian for the past 5 years

conditions.mother: Epilepsy (diagnosed in her 30s, well-controlled on medication), Migraines

personality: scared and embarrassed, keeps apologizing. Normally creative, articulate, and a bit perfectionistic. Very worried about what this means for her driving and her job.
communication_style: Initially post-ictal and giving short, disjointed answers. As she clears, becomes more coherent and asks many questions: 'Does this mean I have epilepsy?' 'Can I still drive?' 'Will this happen again?' Becomes tearful when discussing the incontinence. Wants her boyfriend called.
pain_description_style: Focuses more on the emotional distress than physical pain. Describes headache as 'throbbing' and body aches as 'like I worked out for hours.' Rates pain when asked but is more concerned about the implications of the seizure itself.

critical_findings: First-time generalized tonic-clonic seizure in a 32-year-old with no prior history
Witnessed event with classic tonic-clonic description, tongue bite, and urinary incontinence confirming true seizure
Family history of epilepsy (mother and maternal uncle) - strong genetic risk factor
Elevated prolactin (42) drawn within 30 minutes supports generalized seizure diagnosis
Negative urine drug screen and ethanol level rule out substance-related seizure
Normal electrolytes and glucose rule out metabolic causes
Post-ictal confusion pattern consistent with generalized seizure
Elevated CK (450) from sustained muscle contractions
Takes fluoxetine - SSRIs can lower seizure threshold at higher doses
rubric.diagnosis: New Onset Generalized Tonic-Clonic Seizure (unprovoked, likely new-onset epilepsy given family history)
differential_diagnoses: Provoked seizure (metabolic, toxic - less likely given normal labs)
Syncope with convulsive movements (less likely given witnessed tonic-clonic activity and tongue bite)
Brain tumor or structural lesion (must rule out with imaging)
Cerebral venous thrombosis (young woman on OCPs)
Psychogenic non-epileptic seizure (less likely given objective findings)
recommended_interventions: MRI brain with and without contrast (preferred over CT for first seizure workup)
CT head without contrast if MRI not immediately available
EEG (routine, ideally within 24 hours; consider prolonged monitoring)
Seizure precautions: padded side rails, bed in lowest position, suction at bedside
Discuss antiepileptic medication initiation (levetiracetam is first-line, well-tolerated)
Driving restrictions counseling (state-specific laws, typically 3-12 months seizure-free)
Evaluate fluoxetine dose and seizure threshold implications
Education: seizure first aid, safety precautions, when to call 911
Observation period (minimum 6-12 hours) before discharge consideration
